spec_match.rationale: Uses navigator.cpuPerformance as the initial tier signal and treats Compute Pressure as dynamic state on top.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP clicked tiers 0 through 4 and observed video quality, effects, and AI routing changes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still demonstrates the WICG video-call and AI-routing use case described by the feature index.

edge_cases.rationale: A simulated and optional live PressureObserver path now downgrades a tier-4 session to tier-2 behavior under critical pressure.

references.rationale: Cites ChromeStatus, the WICG CPU Performance draft, and the WICG repository.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables for colors and explicit button background/color pairs.

summary: Quality Tier Selector now reads the correct CPU attribute and layers Compute Pressure simulation/live observation over the base tier. Chrome DevTools MCP verified critical pressure downgrading tier 4 to effective tier 2 with no console errors.
